trend_name: How to care for your skin after a heated workout
description: As much as prep matters, the best thing you can do is cleanse your skin as soon as possible after a heated workout to remove sweat, bacteria, and debris. Use a gentle, hydrating cleanser so as not to further strip the surface of your skin. “From there, focus on restoring the skin barrier with gentle, hydrating ingredients likehyaluronic acid, ceramides, andniacinamide,” Dr. Queller says.
article_title: Heated Workouts Can Affect Your Skin and Hair—Here’s How to Protect Both

description: If you’reacne-prone, Dr. Queller recommends using a mild salicylic acid cleanser a few times per week to help keep your pores clear. “For those prone to redness or sensitivity, calming ingredients like aloe vera or thermal spring water can be helpful,” she says. “The key is to avoid over-exfoliating as your skin is already in a sensitized state after heat exposure.”
article_title: Heated Workouts Can Affect Your Skin and Hair—Here’s How to Protect Both

trend_name: How to protect your hair during a heated workout
description:
Your skin isn’t the only thing losing moisture during a hot yoga class or post-gym sauna session. “Heat and sweat can stress the hair and scalp, and repeated exposure can lead to dryness, frizz, and breakage—particularly in color-treated or chemically processed hair,” Dr. Queller says.
“I recommend applying a lightweight leave-in conditioner or a nourishing oil to create a protective barrier and minimize moisture loss,” Dr. Queller says. As for which hairstyles to wear to class? Skip the slick-backs. Experts say to opt for loose buns and braids, and to choose soft fabric scrunchies over high-tension styles and tight elastic bands, which can cause tension, friction, and breakage.
article_title: Heated Workouts Can Affect Your Skin and Hair—Here’s How to Protect Both

trend_name: T3 Aire 360 Ceramic Air Styler Blowout Kit
description:
If you love the Dyson Airwrap but cringe at the price tag, T3 is the answer. Although it’s about half the price of the Dyson, we found that the Aire 360 works just as well—if not better. In fact, we picked it as ourbest hairstyling tool, period. When you turn it on, the air-powered tool picks the hair up and wraps it around the wand to create blowout-style curls without heat damage. In our evaluations, curls were sleek and shiny, which the brand says is thanks to the CeraGloss ceramic surface of the curlers. Testers toldGlamourthat using this hair tool felt like a luxury experience from start to finish. It comes in a beautiful hexagonal box that opens to reveal a pouch for storage, rather than a bulky, hard-to-store box like the one other similar tools come in. This hair curler itself is lightweight and easy to use.
Glamoursenior commerce editorJenifer Callewho has thick, coarse hair says:
“I have thick, coarse hair, so curling it takes forever, which is why this tool is perfect for quick and easy styling in the morning. Since your hair has to be damp for it to work, I use it after my hair wash day and get gorgeous waves. You can customize to make the curls tighter by using smaller sections. And the curls last. The next day I still wake up with wavy strands. Pro tip: It’s okay to leave it on for 10–15 seconds and not worry about damaging your hair since it’s regulated heat versus intense direct heat,” says Calle.
Glamourcontributor Earley who has wavy hair says:
“Everything from the unboxing to the end style is super sophisticated,” says Earley. “It was super intuitive—you simply use the wand head with the proper arrow, so you can curl both sides away from your face without struggle,” says Earley. “I couldn’t believe that I was able to get such a sleek style on my very long hair in less than 15 minutes.”
article_title: This Is the Only Curling Iron I’ll Use On My Fine, Thin Hair

trend_name: BaByliss Pro 1" Nano Titanium Ultra-Thin Hair Straightener
description:
If you want a travel straightener that checks every box without breaking the bank, this is it. The BaByliss Pro 1" Nano Titanium is lightweight, gets the job done well, and is slim enough to slip into virtually any bag. It comes in three different sizes—we tested the 1"—and leaves hair smooth, silky, and frizz-free. It was favored among others we tested because it’s the lightest, cancreate gorgeous beachy waves, and has five temperature settings to help control for fine strands.
Glamour’s Kilkeary says:
This is probably one of the most straightforward, easy straighteners I’ve ever used, and I’d highly recommend it if you’re new to straightening your hair on the regular. There are five heat settings—I usually switch mine on to the middle temps since I have fine strands—and the plates heat up extremely quickly. My hair overall looks much silkier after using this one, and I’ve been repeatedly reaching for it whenever I wake up with bedhead. —A.M.K.
article_title: This Is the Compact Hair Straightener I Pack Whenever I Travel

description:
If you have long hair, you know the struggle of having to pass a straightener over the same section again and again. The T3 SinglePass StyleMax Professional Flatiron is designed to straighten your hair in one pass, every time. Thanks to its nine heat settings that auto-customize to your specific hair texture and styling goals, the iron is always working at the right temperature to power through your styling goals efficiently. The built-in smart microchip keeps temperature fluctuations in check, so you get maximum smoothing power without frying your ends. All you have to do is input your hair texture into the straightener’s interface, and it will match you with one of the nine heat settings. We recommend running a brush through your hair before using this straightener, as it might not glide through as seamlessly as others on this list. Bonus points for the device’s lightweight, sleek design, which makes it easy to pack in your carry-on.
GlamourcontributorBrigitt Earleysays:
While there is a bit of a learning curve to the smart heat settings, once I figured it out, things came to temperature exceptionally quickly. I appreciated that the rounded edges let you both straighten and curl, but I wish the iron plates swiped more effortlessly; it stuttered over my hair. It did, however, do a pretty solid job in a single pass. —Brigitt Earley,Glamourcontributor
article_title: This Is the Compact Hair Straightener I Pack Whenever I Travel

description:
TheDyson Corraleis one of the few straighteners on the market with a “flight-ready” feature that complies with aviation regulations—meaning you can actually bring it on a plane without any drama. It also works both corded and cordless, so you’re covered whether you’re in a hotel room or touching up on the go—the freedom of being cordless means you don’t need to be dependent on being close to an outlet. This pick does an amazing job of getting you straight hair in just one pass.
Glamourcontributor Gabriella Cuzzone says:
The Corrale heats up wildly fast. It’s nearly instant. It also locks shut, which is really cool. On the lowest heat setting, I had to run the straightener through my hair several times over, so the higher heat worked better for me personally. But being cordless is such a game-changer. You can walk around the house, you can stand back from the mirror, or you can bring it to work. I genuinely loved being able to stand far back from the mirror and not consider where the outlet or cord was. —Gabriella Cuzzone,Glamourcontributor
article_title: This Is the Compact Hair Straightener I Pack Whenever I Travel

trend_name: Hailey Bieber Looks Completely Different With a Platinum Blonde Bob
description:
If Hailey Bieber is reentering herblonde era, she wins the prize for best soft launch. Over the weekend Bieber shared a photo dump of casual selfies, most of them showing her on the go with her son Jack Blues. But one of these shots is not like the others: The fourth image in the carousel shows Bieber with aplatinum blondebob.
Bieber has been committed to hershoulder-length loband glossy chestnut color since December 2025. We last saw it flipped out at the ends for the2026 Vanity Fair Oscars after-partyon March 15. So, naturally, a March 22 bleach blonde selfie leaves us with several questions: Throwback? Teaser? Dye job? All of the above?!
Bieber’s go-to coloristMatt Rezhasn’t shared any evidence of a blonde transformation for Mrs. Bieber…yet. The cut and color in the selfie she shared looks similar to Bieber’s 2020 blonde bob, but her toasty,blurred lipandghost lashesfeel very 2026. Despite the other photos in the post, which show off Bieber’s usual warmbrunette color, consider us inspired.
2020 Vanity Fair Oscars after-party
2026 Vanity Fair Oscars after-party
Even if Hailey Bieber hasn’tgone platinum overnight, a big hair transformation isn’t out of the cards for her. Shortly after her son’s birth in August 2024,she commentedon a fellow postpartum mom’s TikTok: “Let’s hold hands and agree to not make any big hair decisions postpartum! Personally giving myself at least a year!” With Jack’s second birthday coming up this summer, anew colorcould be on the horizon. And in any case, her bright blonde snapshot is great inspo for anyone looking togo lighterfor summer.
WATCH THIS
Tyla Reveals What's On Her Phone
If we know one thing about the model andRhode founder, it’s that she’s always thinking one step ahead. She’s teased product launches withmanicure colorsin the past, so it’s probably safe to say that this platinum déjà vu moment means…something. Until her next post, we’ll be on watch for further proof of bleach.
article_title: Hailey Bieber Looks Completely Different With a Platinum Blonde Bob

trend_name: Texturizing and Volumizing Sea Salt Spray
description: Sun Bum is a brand that specializes in making really greatsunscreensimbued with asignature scentthat has faint hints of banana and coconut, and which they say “smells like summer.” As a compliment to their stellar lineup of SPF, Sun Bum also makes a great Sea Spray, which I've been using for years. I love that it combines high-quality ingredients (sea kelp, black lava sea salt) with an affordable price tag, and I always toss a bottle in mybeach bagas the weather starts to warm.
article_title: The Best Salt Spray Will Give You Beach Hair 365 Days a Year

description: Sea salt sprays are generally contain mostly organic materials, but Jack Henry’s Hair Refresh takes this a step further, usingonlyorganic ingredients. The list includes spring water, sea salt, witch hazel, and lemon oil—and that's it. So, if you're worried about allergies to obscure chemical ingredients or just don't feel like spraying something with numbers in its name on your scalp, this is for you.
article_title: The Best Salt Spray Will Give You Beach Hair 365 Days a Year

description: The supreme Swedish salon staple Sachajuan is your pick for anything grown past your ears; for medium and long styles, and especially for buoyant curls, go with this top-shelf spray. It is one of the more nourishing products you can find in this otherwise drying category, and it gives you a really nice eye-catching natural finish, too.
article_title: The Best Salt Spray Will Give You Beach Hair 365 Days a Year

description: This is the surfer-est choice on the list; for the guy who wants effortlessly tousled beach hair, even if he lives in Wichita. It's a hydrating recipe, too, so when I had shoulder-length hair, this one helped me add volume and gave me separation and definition throughout my mid-lengths and ends without leaving it crunchy, piece-y, and frizzy. Plus, it's pretty affordable compared to other options on this list.
article_title: The Best Salt Spray Will Give You Beach Hair 365 Days a Year

description:
Senior commerce editor Ian Burke uses this SF1 Sea Salt Foam from Patrick's whenever he's not wearing a hat. “It's technically not aspray,” he says, “but it smells incredible and is easy to work into my medium-long naturally curly hair after a shower.”
Shear Revival
article_title: The Best Salt Spray Will Give You Beach Hair 365 Days a Year

description: Dr. Kilgour adds that some derms also offer red light systems in the office. While they tend to offer more consistent parameters, they’re more expensive and less convenient, so top-tier devices for at-home use on the scalp get his stamp of approval.
article_title: Can Red Light Therapy Stop You from Going Bald?

description: Living Proof’s new Perfect Hair Day Shampoo proves that investing in a better shampoo will pay follicular dividends over time. The niche-luxe haircare brand’s latest sudser is a stellar all-rounder for all hair types and a significant upgrade from your standard fare. The foam action is next level; just a small amount lathered like crazy, leaving my hair silky, clean, and with that “run-your-hands-through-it” commercial-esque softness. It’s powered by their Sili-CLONE HairTech, which delivers shine and smoothness without the buildup or dulling you’d get from actual silicone, and it’s gentle on natural oils, too. While thehair stays cleaner longerclaims may vary, I did notice my hair felt fresh well into the next day. The scent and texture have that clinical-shiny familiarity—but here it’s sulfate-free, non-stripping, and housed in a tactile, handsome tube that takes up minimal tub-side real estate. Kinda perfect all around, indeed.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description: From the no-frills packaging to the targeted blends and salon-grade insider vibes, we’re a full-on Davines fan club. There are plenty of repair shampoos out there, but for a gentle, no-fuss formula that does what it promises, NOUNOU is downright reliable. Damage can mean many things—over-treatment, seasonal dryness, brittle ends, or that lesser shampoo not on this list that’s been drying your strands out. A hairstylist friend recommended this one for its repair qualities, and I’m guessing NOUNOU is short for “nourishing,” because that’s what this viscous formula delivers. It works across all hair types and leans on a few soft-spoken, niche ingredients to boost elasticity and replenish moisture with deep, comforting hydration, soothing stressed-out strands with its conditioning-treatment-meets-shampoo vibe.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description: Act+Acre’s latest Daily Hydro Hydrating Shampoo is a mega-moisturizing formula built for hair that needs extra hydration TLC, targeting both the scalp and the hair shaft. It’s formulated with hyaluronic acid (yep, the same hero from your face cream) to replenish moisture, plus Tilicine (their proprietary plant-powered extract) to lock it all in. The lather is rich and creamy, and you can actually feel it infusing your strands as you suds up, with hydration that lingers long after rinsing. It also comes housed in a cool, beaker-esque pump in a punchy blue that looks great in the shower. You may not reach for this every single day, but when cold temps leave your hair tight, thirsty, and in need of moisture, this targeted powerhouse is what you want on deck.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description: With decades of expertise under its belt and a track record of seriously impressive formulas, Aveda is an OG in the shampoo game. I don’t have curly hair, but plenty of my friends do, and while tons of shampoosclaimto cater to curls, Aveda’s Be Curly Shampoo wears its credentials on its sleeve. Curly hair dries out faster than straight hair, so hydration is everything. This formula is packed with a lush seed butter (giving subtle “body cream” vibes) to nourish and condition, plus plant-powered ingredients that fight frizz without disrupting your natural pattern. The rich, thicker texture moisturizes, saturates, and softens on contact, working with your curls and amplifying the shape, bounce, and definition curly-haired folks are lucky enough to flaunt.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description: Luxury haircare stalwart Oribe has a shampoo for every texture, but its brand-new densifying formula is clutch if you’ve got straight hair that sometimes falls flat. The Oribe team even consulted a trichologist to craft a wash that gives straight strands exactly what they crave: density, lift, and a natural sheen without the weight. The blend includes pea sprout extract to support follicle density, red clover extract to help reduce shedding, and peptides to strengthen and fortify. After a few uses, my finer, straighter strands felt denser, fuller, and noticeably shinier. For thicker straight hair, it adds depth; for finer straight hair, it’s a no-brainer.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description:
Wonder Valley
The first thing you’ll notice about California-cool grooming brand Wonder Valley’s shampoo is that it smells refreshingly natural—none of that synthetic, mass-produced stuff. The second is how simple and recognizable the ingredient list is, leaning heavily on oils (cedarwood, grapefruit, frankincense, lime, yuzu, plus a mix of leaf extracts), which give it a natural simplicity. The silky formula doesn’t erupt into a dramatic lather; instead, it forms a subtle, creamy foam that gently cleanses and helps detoxify buildup. There’s a faintly old-world vibe to it: no flashy bells or whistles, just clean, grounding, Mother-Nature-coded nourishment. And we could all use that from time to time.
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

description: A little goes a long way with this incredibly high-foaming shampoo from Highland. It comprises only ingredients that are good for your hair, so even if you're a daily shampooer (guilty as charged) you won't risk overdrying or damaging your hair in the long run. But seriously, maybe consider going a day or two between washes?
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

trend_name: What to Look for in a Shampoo, Based on Hair Type
description:
The first step to finding the right shampoo (and any hair products,body wash, or skin care product, TBH) is to understand your “variables." This means your strand’s overall texture (so, it’s curl or wave pattern, or lack thereof), as well as the individual strand density, the oil levels in your scalp, any sensitivities, etc. Finding a shampoo with active ingredients that match your needs—and learning theright cadence of using it—is crucial for healthy hair. “I recommend everyone tailor their routine to what works best for them and not just use products that are popular at the time,” Lee adds. “Whether you have short or long hair, yourscalp healthis important to focus on and make sure you give it what it needs." Celebrity groomerDan Perri(with A-list clients like Post Malone, Gerard Butler, and Brendan Fraser) adds that men's hair needs and variables might even change as time goes on—aside from just natural thinning. “If you are someone who’s had the same shampoo for years it, may be time for a switch,” he says.
For further evidence of why this is important, here is insight from Lee and Perri into how different textures, lengths, and oil levels impact your daily shampoo needs, plus advice on what kind of product to seek with each variable. And remember, you may have to combine variables (like, if you havecurly hairor straight hair, you will also need to consider if it is coarse and thick, or fine and fragile, as well as oil levels or dryness).
article_title: The Best Shampoo for Men, According to Experts

trend_name: Get strategic haircuts as it grows
description:
It may seem like backwards thinking, but you need to get haircuts as your hair grows out—particularly if you expect it to look presentable along the way. Think of the upside: You get to wear all kinds of hairstyles en route to the end goal. Moreover, it needs to be trimmed and texturized in order to look good. And a haircut need not be drastic; it just needs to be strategic, so that it looks good without compromising overall progress. (The same goes forgrowing out your beard, too.)
Secondly, this strategic trimming will help layer the hair to continue growing in the way you want it to. You can cut the back in a way that allows the top to pour over it, until it’s time to let it all grow in unison. (This will spare you from having a mullet along the way.)
Lastly, the barber or stylist will trim the ends ever so slightly, just to manage any splitting or breaking.
article_title: Here’s the Right Way to Grow Your Hair Out

description:

Dry shampoo:For the days between washes, spray dry shampoo at the roots of your hair to soak up excess oil and give everything added volume. It makes your hair more textured and lively, instead of limp and greasy.
article_title: Here’s the Right Way to Grow Your Hair Out

description: Blow dryer:Helps volumize hair or activate styling products, to magnify their effectiveness. Get an ionic one to minimize heat damage, and get a diffuser and concentrator attachment for curls and root targeting (respectively).

description:
If you want an all-in-one device for hair, body, and face (or some combo of the three), the Braun Series 9 has you covered. Or, well, trimmed. As a high-end beard trimmer and facial hair detailer first and foremost, it has a high-powered motor with the oomph to cut through thick patches anywhere else with ease. Meanwhile, itsfortydifferent trimming settings, all in 0.5-millimeter increments controlled by the dial, ensure you’re not stuck making your sideburns the same length as your chest hair.
Of the Series 9’s myriad attachments, the fine-toothed body-buffer head is a godsend for body hair trimming, letting you mow through thick hair quickly. Not only does it provide a slight buffer between skin and teeth, but since those strands each get combed before the cut, there’s a lot less tugging and clogging than with other multitool-style trimmers.
There are some obvious caveats. For one, the Braun’s not ergonomically designed to be a great back hair trimmer unless you have a very friendly roommate or a very kind partner. And you’ll need to step up your rinsing and disinfecting game if you’re planning on going face-to-balls with the same trimmer. (At the very least, maybe hit the pubesafteryou’ve hit the shower.)
article_title: The Best Body Hair Trimmers Let You Manscape With Ease

description: If you’re ballin’ on a budget, Remington’s All-in-One Groomer is a capable, familiar trimmer that shouldn’t set off any fraud alerts on your credit card. Out of the box, this lightweight grooming tool is comfortable to hold, and the blade head twists into place with a satisfying click. The trimmer’s three fixed guards offer a standard spread of lengths, ranging from 3–9mm; and, for anything in between or beyond, the adjustable comb guard unlocks 10 additional length settings between 2–20mm. We were particularly impressed by the adjustable guard, which proved to be a handy tool for safely fine-tuning short to medium-length body hair with confidence. With additional attachments including a nose and ear trimmer, as well as a refined detail trimmer, the All-in-One Groomer lives up to its name, providing full-body coverage at an affordable price.
article_title: The Best Body Hair Trimmers Let You Manscape With Ease

description:
Shaving your own back is a time-honored chore for the hairiest among us, and the Mangroomer Intellimax Pro brings a home-inventor level of crazy brilliance to a job that otherwise requires extreme contortions. You just unfold it, power it on, drop it over the first shoulder, and get to mowing. The trimming head doesn’t bite or leave marks, but will take hair down to the normal .5mm, while the foil-head shaver attachment will get you smooth-skinned (for maybe a day or two). You can even adjust the length of the Mangroomer’s handle for taller torsos or to get better angles on tricky spots.
It was a lifesaver for me when I was single (before I could sweetly ask my partner to do the job)—and so simple that shaving my back almost became fun. I would use it directly in the shower, making for easy cleanup. (Though never with the shower running; it's waterproof, but I didn't want the handle getting slippery enough that I’d drop it.) I never had any issues with nicking orrazor burnwith the Intellimax Pro’s main trimming head, though it was trickier to get a uniform shave using the foil shaving head. Frankly, it was easier to just use the .5mm trimming head every day if I wanted to stay stubble-free.
article_title: The Best Body Hair Trimmers Let You Manscape With Ease

description:
Any time there’s a blade this close to your important bits, there’s a degree of trust involved. So it’s reassuring that Wahl has been a household name for over a hundred years. I’ve always been a fan of the quality of Wahl products, and our testing revealed the Wahl Manscaper Black Ops to be a consistent and dependable trimmer, equipped to handle all manner of manscaping. Right out of the box, the Black Ops inspires confidence with its well-weighted, stainless-steel build—wrapped in a grippy rubber handle to eliminate any chance of a slip. While it may sound like something you’d download for your PS5, the Manscaper Black Ops isn’t playing any games when it comes to getting the job done. The trimmer arrives with eight guards ranging from 1.5–13 millimeters, as well as an adjustable guide comb to give you more options for your hair down there. And, like its amphibious namesake, the Manscaper Black Ops is fully waterproof.

article_title: The Best Body Hair Trimmers Let You Manscape With Ease

description:
How do you maintain a body hair trimmer?
“If you want the best performance out of your trimmer, keeping the blade clean and lubricated is key,” says Perri. “Oiling the trimmer every few weeks will keep the blade running smooth, prevent any pulling of the hair, and keep the blade temperature down.” He adds that most trimmers come with a small supply of mineral oil—use it. (Wahl sells agreat 4-oz clipper oilfor cheap, too.) Be sure to use a machine-safedisinfectant sprayto help keep the blade sanitized, which will prevent breakouts, Perri adds.
Can you use a beard trimmer for manscaping?
Some beard trimmers are designed for use on the body, but only if they have attachments designed to keep skin from being pulled into the blade. Keep in mind that the ergonomics of a great beard trimmer doesn’t necessarily align with the ergonomics you’ll want for shaving body parts. You’ll have a harder time hitting hard to reach places, like the back. And beyond that, you’ll want to aggressively sanitize any trimming heads and guards that touch both your face and your…[looks down].
What is the best body hair trimmer for men?
AsGQ's lead grooming product reviewer, I firmly believe that best body hair trimmer for most people is theBraun All-in-One Series 9 Trimmer 9440, with its innovative vertical blade that allows for easier maneuvering. You can read more about it in the review above.
How often should I trim my body hair?
Depends! If you want to be as smooth as an Olympic swimmer, you might want to shave every day. If that's too much of a hassle, doing it every two or three days can be sufficient to have you smooth without your stubble scratching up your shirts.
Should I shave off all of my body hair?
Do you find body hair unsightly? Then maybe you want to shave your body hair to give yourself a smoother appearance. The decision to trim body hair usually depends on what you or even your partner prefers. For professional sports, a hairless body might be preferred for an advantage, like for swimmers or wrestlers.
Is it better to trim body hair when it's wet or dry?
You can shave your hair whether it's wet or dry, but we prefer doing it on dry hair. Trimmers are able to more easily cut through the hair when it's dry, and you can get more even cuts if you're not going for a clean shave. (Though wet/dry trimmers are all over the market.)
What are common manscaping mistakes?
Besides using non-skinsafe grooming electricals (we got you covered in that department), some common manscaping mishaps are the result of poor technique (shaving against the grain), not trimming on cleansed, exfoliated skin, and neglecting post-shaving moisturizing—especially if you have sensitive skin.
How do I avoid razor burn?
Razor burnis no joke. Preparing the skin, using proper shaving technique, and including lotions, moisturizers, and other skin car products into you grooming routine are all good ways to prevent razor burn.
Dan Perriis a celebrity groomer based between NYC, LA, and Florida. His A-list clients include Post Malone, Gerard Butler, and Brendan Fraser.
article_title: The Best Body Hair Trimmers Let You Manscape With Ease

trend_name: A Guide to Curtain Fringes for Mature Hair
description:
Like with any fringe, an expert eye and professional hand are key when it comes to achieving a look that suits your face shape effortlessly.
“The key with curtain bangs on mature hair is softness and proportion," says Cassidy. "You want them to start no shorter than the cheekbone and taper gently into the length. Anything too short or too heavy can feel ageing.”
He emphasises the importance of texture, too; a slightly broken-up finish stops them looking flat and gives a much more relaxed, lived-in effect.
“Before committing to curtain bangs, think about your natural hair texture and growth pattern," he adds. “Cowlicks, partings, and how your hair falls when it air-dries all matter. A good stylist will tailor the fringe to work with your hair, not against it. The beauty of curtain bangs is their versatility. When done well, they grow out seamlessly and can be worn parted, swept back, or tucked away.”
article_title: Why a Curtain Fringe Is the Most Flattering Trend for Mature Hair

trend_name: How to Style a Curtain Fringe for Mature Hair
description:
Before you veer away from your signature everyday style, it's important to consider how much additional styling time a new fringe might require.
“Curtain bangs are surprisingly low-maintenance if they’re cut properly,” says Cassidy. “A medium round brush or even a large Velcro roller at the front while the hair cools will give that soft sweep away from the face.”
He recommends styling your curtain bangs with the hairdryer angled side-to-side rather than straight down. “It creates lift without stiffness and keeps your bangs looking light and flattering.”
article_title: Why a Curtain Fringe Is the Most Flattering Trend for Mature Hair

description:
Meri Kate O’Connor, a celebrity colorist atBENJAMIN Salonin Los Angeles, says that clients are looking to go “lighter and brighter, moving toward more of an all-over blond rather than the sun-kissed, lived-in balayage look.” She looks to Margot Robbie, Elsa Hosk, and Elle Fanning as inspiration for that bright blond. It’s less beachy than past iterations.
Fortiz says Jenna Perry Hair Salon has been seeing an influx of the light beige, pearly “champagne blond”—and there has been an eight percent increase in Google searches for the color in 2026. “It’s very reflective—never ashy,” she explains of the luminous, sparkly hue.
Then, Baum says her clients are asking for bright golden-blond colors with more natural-looking roots (further emphasizing the desire for a lower-maintenance blond hair color without going “bronde”). “I think blondes have been so focused on achieving cooler tones in the past, but lately it’s nice to see people leaning back into the warmth of their natural color,” she adds. “That bright, sparkly blond is really having a moment.”
The bottom line on blond: stylists are avoiding ashy, cool tones and focusing on brighter colors all over, rather than piecey balayage dye jobs.
article_title: The New Rules of Blond Hair

description:
Most stylists agree that full highlights two to three times a year is the magic number, with touch-ups every two to four months. However, this is all dependent on how much growth you’re comfortable with, Baum adds. Since health is now more important than ever, techniques like partial highlights and glosses can help maintain color without damaging hair. Experts even recommend at-home glosses but urge you to avoid at-home touch-ups or full dye jobs. “Never bleach your hair at home,” Baum warns. “We all know that one friend who tried and ended up with orange hair—don’t be that friend!”
At-home products can also help maintain your hair's color and health. Anyone who has been blond knows about the benefits of a purple shampoo—when used in moderation, experts say. Fortiz warns that using purple shampoo can make blond hair look duller and lifeless because it’s muting the reflection. Instead, opt for color-safe shampoos and conditioners to keep the color vibrant and bright, and lock in the tone, Baum says.
Both O’Connor and Fortiz recommend using detox and clarifying shampoos every one to two weeks (again, the key is not to overdo it) to prevent mineral buildup that can dull your blond color. Lastly, hydration is key to maintaining color and tone, so add a mask to your routine at least once a week.
article_title: The New Rules of Blond Hair

trend_name: What Makes Greige Highlights So Flattering?
description:
“Greige is the ultimate soft landing between blonde and gray. It lets you embrace your natural tone while keeping that luminous, expensive-looking finish,” saysSibi Bolan, senior colorist atGeorge Northwood’s Holland Parksalon.
“By blending cool beige and soft silver hues, you get a seamless transition that feels fresh and modern.”
article_title: Greige Highlights Are Winter Coolest Color Trend

trend_name: Who Are Greige Highlights Best Suited For?
description:
AsMarley Xavier, celebrity colorist atHershesons Belgravia, adds, it’s ideal for those who have been bleaching their hair for so long that they can barely remember their natural color. “Greige highlights offer a cool-neutral tone that softly connects to regrowth, lending luminosity to gray hair while reducing harsh contrast at the root. It makes the grow-out feel intentional.”
If you’re wondering if the look would suit you, the beauty of greige is that it encompasses a broad spectrum of shades. “Greige is incredibly adaptable,” says Bolan. “Warmer skin tones suit creamier, beige-based shades, while cooler complexions shine withsilver or ash-beige blends. The key is finding that perfect neutral that enhances your skin - it should feel like it belongs to you.”
Andbrunettesshouldn’t rule out greige either. Bolan says she opts for muted mushroom or smoky taupe tones through the mid-lengths and ends when dealing with darker hair: “It creates that same chic, lived-in feel, while keeping the richness in the hair,” she adds.
article_title: Greige Highlights Are Winter Coolest Color Trend

description:
As always, finding a colorist who understands your vision for your hair is key. Bolan says that a good starting point for those transitioning from blonde to gray would be a ratio of around 60 percent beige-blonde hair to 40 percent gray or silver. “As more natural gray grows in, we can gradually shift that balance,” she adds. Ultimately, greige gives you the space to take the transition as slowly or quickly as you like.
As with all hair color, it’s a personal journey. “Some may wish to keep brighter blonde around the face to retain warmth and familiarity, while introducing cooler, silvered tones subtly through the crown or nape,” Xavier suggests. “Others may choose to follow their natural gray pattern, especially if white or silver streaks are emerging, enhancing these with alabaster or Scandi blonde accents.”
He recommends techniques like soft blending, foil-free placement, or fine babylights to keep things looking natural and avoid harsh contrasts. “Ultimately, greige is a process, not a one-off appointment,” adds Xavier. “It’s a tonal dialogue between your natural hair and your evolving style, and it should always make you feel more yourself, not less.”
article_title: Greige Highlights Are Winter Coolest Color Trend

trend_name: What Part Looks Better for My Face Shape?
description:
Play around with your hair part and see what you like best. According to Simmonds, a middle part “elongates and flatters balanced face shapes, while side parts suit round and square faces, and suit curls beautifully.”
You can also change it up according to your mood. “I like to part my hair both ways, and, in general, wearing a middle part or a side part is flattering on most face shapes,” says Puncerelli. “A middle part definitely gives off a little bit more modernity, and a side part is more classic.” What you’re wearing plays a role, too. “Some looks are better with a middle part, some look sharper when your hair is parted to the side,” she says.
article_title: Should You Wear a Center Part or a Side Part?

description:
If nourishing your hair was important before, it’s even more important as we age. “Gray hair needs that because there are textural changes that are inherently associated with the lack of pigment and the lack of the disulfide bonds,” explains Small, referring to the chemical bonds that maintain the structure of hair’s keratin protein.
Small says he views gray hair like a balloon that’s starting to deflate. “I want to fill it up with moisture and nourishing ingredients,” he explains.
Luckily, there are plenty of products that can help in this area: “Rich conditioners, weekly masks, and a good leave-in are key,” says Santiago. Her favorite?AGE beautiful Hydrating Mask, which "rehydrates and moisturizes wiry, coarse silver hair."
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

description: “I think many of us have adopted a more robust skincare routine as we've gotten older, and then shampoo and conditioner kind of remains the same,” says Small. He stresses the importance of a basic knowledge of ingredients and formulas. “If you have gray hair that's coarse or dry or brittle, if something's too cleansing, too stripping, that can be a very easy place to start,” he says. “There are now a lot of ingredients within shampoo that we can look for with antioxidants andpeptides that will nourish the scalpas well.”
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

description: Cutting your hair is one of the best ways to ensure hair health, no matter your color. Plus, it just helps gray hair look polished. “Regular trims and gentle styling can help prevent frizz and maintain an intentional look,” says Santiago.
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

description: UV rays also affect your hair, and of course, your scalp. “Don’t forget sunscreen on your part,” reminds Santiago. “Gray hair reflects more UV, and the scalp burns faster.” For extra credit, wear a hat when you're in direct sunlight.
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

description: Gray hair tends to have a different texture and finish than other types of hair, so don’t be surprised if your styling routine needs a rethink, too. And that might mean more heat-styling (with proper prep and heat protection, of course): “If you used to wear your hair natural and let it dry naturally, you might need to polish it a little bit more,” says Small.
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

trend_name: Use a Purple Shampoo and Conditioner
description: Gray color can look dull, too—and that’s where a purple shampoo can work wonders to brighten it up. Santiago stresses to “Protect hair from the sun, use neutralizing shampoos, and add a clear or silver gloss for shine” for bright gray or white color, recommending Clairol Shimmer Lights to get the job done.
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

description:
If graying or thinning happens suddenly, it might be time to check in with your doctor in addition to your trusted hairstylist. “What graying indicates is that there's a change happening in the body,” says Small. “Typically, we look at it as a barometer—whether it's nutrition, active stress, general age, stress in general, sleeping, et cetera.”
Santiago concurs, adding that “managing stress, supporting your nutrition, and avoiding chronic heat or harsh chemicals can slow the process and keep your hair healthy longer.”
article_title: The Expert Guide to Caring for Gray Hair, From the Best Shampoos to Styling Secrets

Long layers are forever. This is especially true in the transitional styling sense. Regardless of your preferred length, long, airy layer are shorn to grow with you, all while making your getting ready routine as seamless as possible.
Rather than short or choppy layers that require some level of commitment, long layers showcase all natural textures with minimal effort. Maintain the specific tiers or let them grow a bit; either way, the simple touch adds visual interest.

Chaie de Jesus, co-founder ofOnyx on Madison, considers acollarbone-length lobthe ultimate means of having your cake and eating it too."Think short and fresh, but long enough to tie back or tuck," she says. Plus, a grow-out will find you landing in safe and chic medium-length territory—and you can always go shorter with a simple trim.

Gilbert Flores/WWD via Getty Images
There's fringe, and then there's fringe that is light, ethereal, and offbeat enough to defy things like seasonality and grow-outs.

Rather than super sleek, heavy, or overly styled, yourwhimsical fringeshould feel soft and carefree, making the detail an ideal choice for a transitional cut. "I love the versatility of it, and the grow-out is seriously so seamless," says de Jesus. The fact that the bixie is among 2026's trending crops makes the choice to chop even easier.
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description:
Stephane Cardinale - Corbis/Corbis via Getty Images

"A long shag worn through winter can grow into spring beautifully," says Bailey-Chaidez. "Perfect for glamorous updos with soft, fringey face-framing pieces left out."
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description:
Amy Sussman/Getty Images
Themicro bobis a baby bob that's destined to grow into its full form—if you're into that. While the micro gets its grit from its super-short shape, rocking it from season to season works even if it gets a little longer.
As a bonus, the micro bob is chic and neat enough for winter, but still weightless enough for warm-weather wear, so keeping things extra short is an excellent option.

description:
Steve Granitz/FilmMagic
A more fringe-forward version of the curtain bang,bottleneck bangsare a bohemian option that leans low-maintenance, a cornerstone of a transitional haircut.
A shorter shape through the middle makes the bottleneck more of a true bang, but long framing pieces mean that the fringe will eventually veer into curtain territory if left untouched.
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description:
John Shearer/WireImage
The cowboy bob is all about body and volume, elements that lend to both winter drama and springtime play. The face-framing style is primed for deep side parts and ample finger-raking, ideal options for the swing from hoods and hats to outdoor revelry.
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description:
Frazer Harrison/Getty Images
The wolf cut isn't going anywhere, but the most recent iteration is a little gentler than versions past. Still present are volume and copious amounts of layers, but cut with a soft, feathered look in mind rather than an intensely edgy one.
Varied lengths mean extra growth won't result in the style's erasure, and the lightweight look is right at home in spring.
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description:
Stephane Cardinale - Corbis/Corbis via Getty Images
TheFrench bobis timeless, seasonless, and destined for grow-out greatness.
De Jesus considers a French bob to be a superior style. "It's perfect when you want polish without heaviness, or if you want to style it with little bends or waves," says de Jesus. Try the chop with any texture and enjoy the luxury that comes with an intentional, yet flexible, style.
article_title: 10 Transitional Haircut Ideas to Take Your Lengths from Winter to Spring

description: The bob has taken over lately, and I'm not mad about it. Even it-girls like Hailey Bieber andMargot Robbieon board. Thompson explains why the collar-grazing length is such a client favorite: "The Riviera Bob feels polished but effortless. The cut sits around the collarbone with fullness through the ends, along with a sweeping bang that moves with the wind of the Riviera. This creates a refined silhouette that still feels modern.”
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: If you're looking for somethingvolume-boosting, opt for a bouncy bob. It can feel counter-intuitive to chop off your lengths, but shorter styles naturally have more spring to them. "It feels light, fresh and full of movement, says Fox. "It's soft volume and face-framing shape also give a polished yet effortless look that works for both everyday wear and dressier occasions.”
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: Glassybrondefocuses on shine and soft dimension rather than bold highlights. Micro ribbons of lighter brunette and blonde reflect light through the hair to create that glossy, dimensional finish. Which is why stars like Jennifer Lopez have been sporting this shade for years on end. Thompson shares her technique: “Sometimes I create that ribbon-like dimension using balayage shades in extensions from Donna Bella Hair. Tape-in extensions can mimic the look of highlights running through the hair, which adds dimension instantly and allows clients to experiment with lighter pieces without committing to permanent color.”
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: The early 2000s relic that's returning? Highlights without the faux paus. “After years of super blended balayage, highlights are starting to make a comeback," says Thompson. "Clients are asking for brighter pieces of lightness that create contrast and movement through the hair. Instead of everything melting together, highlights bring back that classic dimensional look where the brightness actually pops.”
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: According to Thompson, we're entering the density era. Clients are aiming for thicker hair, no matter their length. “Extensions used to be associated with very long hair, but now clients are using them at every length," she says. "Whether it’s a bob, mid-length cut, or long layers, adding density makes the haircut look more polished and intentional.” To recreate, she builds that fullness using hybrid wefts that adds density through the mid-lengths and ends, without creating bulk at the root. This way, your hair looks thick, but still natural and movable.
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: Color Technique is just as essential. For a softly diffused, dimensional blonde, request delicate light mapping from your stylist. The effect is subtle yet striking–ultra-fine highlights are woven into the hair so they almost seem imperceptible. The result is a perfectly-place halo of light that illuminates your face.
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: For me, Bella Hadid is the slick back blueprint (rightfully so!). This sleek bun style is perfect for spring because it looks clean, elegant, and keeps hair off the face, making it ideal for warmer temps. To achieve this, you'll need some strong hair ties and a trusty bristle brush to start. Fox always reaches for theMaria Nila Extreme SprayandFinishing Waxto keep everything in place. Bonus: it'sthego-to for making dirty hair work or if you're in a rush.
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

description: An oldie but a goodie, soft beachy waves will always be a staple. The effortless style matched the relaxed and airy vibe of the season, whether you're on a coastal vacay or not. "The natural movement and sun-kissed texture also add volume and dimension making hair feel fresh and full of life after winter," says Fox. "For this look, my go-to is theMaria Nila Texture Sprayto keep the wave in place but give the hair a sea salt feel.”
article_title: These 10 Hairstyles Will Be Top-Tier for Spring, According to Stylists

As someone who didn'tcolor my hairfor the first time until I was thirty years old, I truly admire the way that Halsey is able to flawlessly switch between differenthair colorsand styles, and I'm even more impressed that she's able to make every single one of them—even her current jet black color—look so chic.
On April 3, the singer andabout-facefounder accompanied her partner, Avan Jogia, to a Los Angeles screening of his new film,Our Hero, Balthazar. She showed up to the event wearing a black, satin midi dress with cap sleeves andlace detailingaround the bust and neckline, plus a moody newhair colorto really lean into the all-black look for the night. For nearly two months, Halsey has been wearing her hair in a shortmullet haircutthat's shaved down on both sides and features tousledbaby bangs, and after previously attending the film's New York premiere event in March with her hair dyed a vibrant burnt orange color, she's switched things up again this time with jet black hair.
Halsey wears a jet black mullet to a screening of "Our Hero, Balthazar" on April 3.
Over the last several months, Halsey has played around with a variety of different hair colors, styles, and lengths. Last summer, for example, she dyed her bob bright blue. A few weeks later she cut her hair even shorter tomicro boblength and addedbaby bangsand neon yellow dye to the bottom half of her head in a peekaboo style. By December, the singer attended the Los Angeles premiere ofMarty Supremewith bleachedblonde hairthat was cut even shorter to apixie cut.
Black hair is definitely tame compared to the shades she's worn in the past, but it's shockingly on trend for spring. "Velvety black tones are making a comeback, but with more shine and softness so the color never looks flat," celebrity hairstylistLaurie Heapspreviously toldMC.
If getting a short haircut or debuting a new hair color (or both!) is on your list of things to do this spring, read ahead for tips on styling and maintenance.
Keep your color from fading with a color-safe shampoo.
The best thing you can do for color-treated hair is ensure that it stays moisturized. Use a conditioner that'll protect the vibrancy of your color.
Follow up with a leave-in product that'll hydrate and prevent breakage.
Use thisglossafter shampooing to give your hair some extra shine.
Give short hair a tousled, lived-in look with a styling balm.

Zendayaofficially debuted her bixie haircut duringParis Fashion Weekafter secretly getting the chop shortly before Christmas, and since then, it's already lived several lives. She's gone from wearing it in its naturally curly state to giving it a sleek and chic style at the98th Academy Awards. Now she's finally giving it the hydro bixie treatment.
The 29-year-old joined her co-stars at the Los Angeles premiere of the long-awaited third season of HBO'sEuphoriaon April 7, whereshe wore a backless halter gownfrom Ashi Studio that came in a richchocolate brown color. She's fresh off the press tour for her new film,The Drama, where she hit four red carpet events wearing dramatic hair and makeup looks that included ablue smokey eyeand dark"siren eyes"makeup, so it was only right that she keep the momentum going at Tuesday night's event. On her eyes, she wore warm eyeshadow with black eyeliner while her hair was given a glossy, almost-wet texture, courtesy of her hairstylistUrsula Stephen.
"We wanted to play off the texture we did for the last carpet, but a little tougher," Stephen said in a statement. "We started with clean, wet hair and blew the hair away from the face."
Zendaya wears a hydro bixie to the season three premiere of "Euphoria."
Hydro bobsand wet hairstyles don't seem to be going anywhere for spring 2026. Earlier this year, hairstylistGregory Pattersonpredicted that the wet look would be one of the year's most requested red carpet hairstyles. “Think of creating a really high-gloss polish that is intentionally shine-enhanced through finish products, but without looking overly wet,” he previously toldMC.
Stephen also gave a little more insight into how she achieved wet and wavy style onZendaya, including a few of the products she used. Read ahead to get the look.
Stephen started off by usingblow dry brushthat dries the hair without using an extreme amount of hair.
Next, she combined this soft cream with a light oil to give the hair its wet look.
Use a detangling brush like this one to work the product in and evenly distribute it.
A lightweighthair oilwill also give you a glossy, shiny finish.
Give yourself lightly-tousled look with a hair lotion.

description:
Before I apply anything slightly heavy, I like to go in with light leave-in products (after conditioning in the shower, of course).
Hyaluronic acidcan be just as moisturizing for your hair as it is for your skin and body. It’s the star ingredient in this amika product, and it has a really soft, milky texture that my hair drinks right up.
I’ll be a Briogeo loyalist forever, especially when it comes to the Farewell Frizz collection. As someone who spends a lot of time trying tocombat frizzand dryness, I’m a fan of this product because it’s a lightweight, non-messy spray that organizes and reduces frizz with oils like argan, rosehip, and coconut.
article_title: My Curly Hair Is Obsessed With This Luxurious Hair Butter

description:
Now for the main attraction: the hair butters. Having curly hair means that I often have to deal with fairy knots andtangleswhen I wear my hair in its natural state, which usually happens when it’s not moisturized enough. To prevent that (as well as the brittle feeling that my hair gets just days after washing andblow drying), I like to incorporate butters into my routine after applying my leave-ins. These are just a handful of my favorites.
Innersense already has a stellar lineup of curl products, but this creamy, whipped texturizer (it’s not anactualtexturizer that will loosen your curls) does a great job of keepingfrizzto a minimum while keeping the curls defined.
This one is a favorite amongnatural hairgurus for a reason. It has a thick formula that moisturizes but it doesn’t ever feel like it’s sitting on top of the hair making it greasy. A small amount is enough to soften the hair while giving it ample moisture.
This butter-gel hybrid can be used on hair that’s freshly washed, or you can use it to spruce up hair and styles that are a few days old. It’s made withaloe, which hydrates severely dry hair while also calmingitchinessand inflammation on the scalp.
"Butter" being in the name of a hair product might immediately make you think that the product itself is thick and greasy, but that's not always the case, especially with this cream. It uses a light amount of avocado oil and aloe to moisturize the hair, and it can be applied to hair straightaway after washing or when it's dried or blown-out.
This is more of a scalp butter, but as we've all come to learn, a healthy,hydrated scalpis just as important as healthy strands. Cécred's formula is ideal for applying to the scalp with your hair is in protective styles likecornrowsandbox braids, but you can also use it when your hair is in its natural state.
As I Am Doublebutter Cream adds softness to the hair while also targeting issues like dry,split ends. It's one of my favoritedrugstore styling options, and the $10 price is one you just can't beat.
article_title: My Curly Hair Is Obsessed With This Luxurious Hair Butter

trend_name: “It’s Going to Be So Different”: Katseye on Going Big—and Going Blonde!—for Coachella
description:
There are few things more frustrating than a major technical issue at the start of an interview. That’s all that was happening for the first 15 minutes I spent withKatseye. Static, a dying laptop battery, frozen cameras, and more plagued our attempt at a video call from the two most different places possible: while the group’s five active members (Daniela Avanzini, Lara Raj, Megan Skiendiel, Yoonchae Jeung, and Sophia Laforteza) are sequestered in a remote dance studio in Los Angeles, rehearsing for their Coachella set on Friday night, I’m at Blackberry Farm in the Tennessee Smoky Mountains.
By the time everybody was coming through loud and clear, the girls are cuddled up on a giant couch in black-and-white dance gear. “We’ve been working for weeks on our performance—9 a.m. to 11 p.m. today—because we want it to be as perfect as possible,” Avanzini says. “We’ve been working on some new things, so it’s going to be very different.”
Music isn’t the only thing they’ll be debuting on the Sahara Stage; last week, the group decided it was time for a makeover, and celebrity hairstylist and Matrix global artistGeorge Papanikolaswas on hand to make it all happen. (Katseye is a global brand ambassador for the brand, as well.) The change began with Jeung, who, in her own words, has “been wanting to go blonde for a while now and finally convinced our visual creative team”—namely, creative director Humberto Leon and visual director João Moraes.
How Aespa’s Giselle Perfects No-Makeup Makeup
She cites Pinterest as an important source of inspiration for her golden-blonde makeover: “I don’t even know who the person is in the inspiration photo, I just knew I wanted the color to have a little bit of yellow,” she says.
From there, it wasn’t long before the rest of the group hopped on the bleach bandwagon, each to totally different ends. “The looks have this natural edge to them because we all have some elements of black hair and some elements of blonde hair, with Yoonchae being completely blonde,” Raj says. That edge, she adds slyly, was “something we wanted to showcase with our new album.”
Raj’s Y2K-style blonde highlights were inspired by another pop music great: Christina Aguilera (or, as Raj calls her, “Xtina”). “The 2000s are an era of music and pop culture that we find really inspiring,” she says. “I’ve always loved the song ‘Dirrty’ and wanted to have those types of streaks in my hair since I was eight. It’s still edgy, but I like that I could keep the black hair around my face and just add in bright little blonde pops.”
It was Olympic gold medalistAlysa Liu’s graphic manethat inspired Skiendiel’s makeover. “We decided to do my own version of her shine lines, which almost looks like you just splashed some bleach on my hair and found out what happened,” she says. In reality, the S-shaped curve was far more intentional: Papanikolas shaped it by hand.
“You can tell I really like to experiment with hair from all of our past eras,” Skiendiel adds, citing a ginger phase and hot pink bangs. But this look felt fresh, cool, and of the moment. “I actually got to meet Alysa right after dyeing my hair like this, and I loved that we really look like twins.”
Avanzini and Laforteza’s looks were a little more subtle. “These are extensions!” Laforteza gushes of her dip-dye look, which almost invertsDebbie Harry’siconic two-tone grunge-glam ’do of the late ’70s. The platinum extensions that peek out from beneath her glossy black hair added major inches without altering her hair routine too much. “I love hair masks and glosses, so I had them darken my natural hair, which I’m just drenching in Matrix hair masks,” Laforteza says. “Then, when it’s time to perform, I just get to add in the extensions, and I feel like Hannah Montana when she puts the wig on. I can take it off and be Miley, be Sophia, be myself. But when I go home, I still get to be myself. It was the perfect way for me to still be a part of the blonde.”
For naturally curly-haired Avanzini—whose spirals have become part of her beauty signature—less was more: two pert platinum money pieces now frame her face. “I’ve been blonde before, and while I miss it, I really love my dark hair,” she says. “This was a way to bring back just a little bit of my past blonde era, but in a subtle way.” She’s excited to style the pieces into braids, an updo, and more.
As we’re chatting, Jeung shares that while she’s obsessed with her makeover, the look is also taking a moment to get used to. “I also chose to bleach my eyebrows,” she says. “So when I don’t have makeup on and walk by a mirror, it’s like seeing an entirely different face.”
Regardless of the extent of the makeover, Papanikolas says that one thing remained top of mind: keeping each girl’s hair healthy. “Any time you’re lightening your hair, you’re putting it through stress,” he says. To combat that, each girl was sent home with theMatrix Build-A-Bond maskand instructions to use it weekly. “It will keep their hair strong and soft, which the girls need because of all the hair choreography.”
If you’re wondering why the weeks before Coachella—one of their most exciting career milestones to date—felt like the right time for a big change, Skiendiel has the answer: “We love to play around with our concepts with every era of Katseye. It’s something that’s really prominent fromSIStoBeautiful Chaos. ‘Pinky Up’ is our third comeback.” Only time—and their Coachella set—will tell what they have in store.
article_title: “It’s Going to Be So Different”: Katseye on Going Big—and Going Blonde!—for Coachella

description:
Now, Robbie has a “lob”—the in-between cut that hits somewhere between the chin and clavicle. And she’s in great haircut company. The specific lob-chop is a favorite of stars like Penelope Cruz (who revealed hers at the Chanel Couture show earlier this year!), Sienna Miller, and more. Robbie’s chop was done by hairstylistCervando Maldonado. Her across-the-forehead bangs are piecey (thus, why they are called “broken”), a style that gives them endless versatility.
“The bob and fringe is a modern look for spring,” Maldonado tells Vogue exclusively of Robbie’s new cut. “It leads the way into summer with an effortless style. The length is versatile and easy to pull back and create different styles if needed.”
We last saw the star with a bob in 2024, where it was styled into a sleek, tucked-in bob. They say hair holds memories, so maybe it was now time to ditch the feelings—the yearning!—associated withWuthering Heights.

description:
Much like skin care, consumers are becoming more informed and more discerning. They’re reading Reddit threads, asking derm-fluencers on TikTok their questions, and actually scanning ingredient lists instead of defaulting to whatever their mom used. The result is a category being held to a much higher standard.
At the same time, global influence is reshaping the market, with brands from Korea, Japan, Brazil, Australia, and even Georgia introducing new formulations and philosophies. Across the board, products are now designed to extend, maintain, and improve hair health, not just mold and maneuver it to our style of choice. From herbal shampoos and bond-building treatments to scalp-focused growth formulas, these are the six trends poised for major 2026 momentum.
In This Story
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

description:
If there’s one category that’s taken over every marketing meeting recently, it’s bond repair. If you’re not entirely sure how it works, celebrity hairstylist Benjamin Mohapi explains that every chemical service breaks down bonds inside the hair shaft, and these treatments are designed to rebuild them. “It’s not a conditioner—it’s working on a structural level,” he explains. “It leads to less breakage, better elasticity, and hair that actually behaves.” In salons, bond builders are now often added into services almost automatically, though they can occasionally affect results depending on the formula. Not everything plays well together, so it’s worth knowing what’s actually being used on your hair.
Still, the results are hard to argue with. With so many brands in the space, there’s something for everyone—notably offerings by Olaplex and K18, which pioneered the category. As Mohapi puts it, “Olaplex No. 3 is the original bond builder for a reason. This isn’t a mask or a conditioner. It’s a pre-shampoo treatment that targets the disulfide bonds broken by coloring and heat. When you pair it with the rest of the system, you’re giving your hair the best possible chance to bounce back.” K18, he says, delivers similar results in a more lightweight format. “I love K18’s repair mask. It’s not just a quick fix, it’s working deep inside the hair shaft to restore elasticity and strength.” Newer entries like Ouai’s repair balm and Maria Nila’s bond builder are only expanding the category further by creating formulas that work for different hair types or use at other parts of one’s regimen.
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

description:
If you scan ingredient lists a little more closely, you’ll notice brands are expanding well beyond the usual paraben-free, sulfate-free formulas that have been around for years, shifting in favor of herbal, plant-based formulations. Celebrity hairstylist Kenna Kennor explains that the interest is part of a broader mindset shift: “People are more conscious of the chemicals they’re using on themselves and in their lives generally, which has spiked interest in herbal alternatives and plant-based hair care,” he says. “There’s a real demand for clean beauty, and brands are responding with formulas that actually work. I’m seeing clients gravitate toward shampoos with ingredients like turmeric, rosemary, and botanical extracts—not just for the wellness aspect, but because they deliver results without stripping the hair.”
There’s a wide range of options, from Ayurvedic-rooted brands like Shaz & Kiks and Ranavat to more luxury entries like Sangre de Fruta. Kennor also points to Aromatica as one to watch. “Everything Aromatica is doing is incredible—even the name of their Rosemary Scalp Scaling Shampoo has helped my clients understand that we’re washing our scalp, not our hair,” he says, adding that shampooing twice on each wash day delivers the best results.
Shaz & Kiks
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

description: You can run, but you can’t hide. Protein is everywhere right now—not just in your diet but in your hair masks too. Keratin—the structural protein responsible for hair, skin, and nails—is showing up in more formulas and is especially effective for frizz, Kennor explains. “Frizz is generally the battle for users of these products, and they work incredibly well at smoothing and strengthening the hair shaft,” he explains. “What I love about the current generation of at-home keratin masks is that they’re formulated without high concentrations of formaldehyde or methylene glycol, focusing instead on safe, conditioning ingredients.” In other words, you’re getting the smoothing benefits without the harsh trade-offs that once defined keratin treatments. As for what to try, he’s seen standout results from Virtue’s Restorative Hydrating Treatment Mask. “A little goes a long way, and in the few minutes it recommends, you get real results. As we come into summer frizz season, this is going to be an essential part of my kit.” He also recommends Ouai’s Fine to Medium Hair Treatment Masque for finer hair types. “I love the smell, and you shouldn’t be afraid to use it on the roots as long as you rinse it well.”
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

description:
Economically, committing to salon color every few weeks just isn’t realistic, especially if your color fades or your hair grows quickly. Thankfully, we now have root touch-up sprays, which have quietly become one of the most useful products on the market. Per Kennor, the formulas have come a long way. “Root touch-up sprays have become incredibly precise with their color matching and, most importantly, the way they reflect light,” he says. “They go virtually unnoticed.” Time is money, and these sprays essentially buy you time. Most can stretch your color by a week or two without looking obvious or chalky. “For clients managing grays or maintaining dimensional color, it’s a game changer,” Kennor says. “It gives them control over their maintenance schedule without sacrificing how polished they look.”
You also don’t have to empty your coffers, as many of the best options are available at drugstores. Kennor loves L’Oréal Paris’s Root Cover Up, especially on set. “It’s invisible under constant light and easy to find, since so many pharmacies stock it.” He also uses Clairol’s Root Touch-Up spray for red tones. “The auburn shade is a perfect match for Britt Lower’s hair color, making it ideal for emergency coverage, especially when she’s filmingSeverance.”
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

description:
Calling all blondes! A hop, skip, and jump frompurple shampoos, purple hair masks are a great option if you want a cooler, more silver finish without sacrificing softness. Mohapi puts it simply: With summer approaching, brassiness is basically inevitable. “Sun, hard water, and time in the city—they all play a part in messing up your color, and a purple mask is the simplest way to deal with it,” he says. “Swap it in for your conditioner once a week, leave it on for a few minutes, and your color will look like you just left the salon again.”
That said, don’t get carried away. “Be careful not to overuse it or leave it on too long,” he adds. “You still want to be blonde, not a shade of violet!” Most formulas are deeply conditioning too, so you’re toning and treating at the same time. It’s not complicated; it just works. There’s no shortage of options, from Kérastase and Amika to Matrix, plus a mask by Christophe Robin that Mohapi considers a favorite: “It’s more than just a toner. It’s deeply nourishing, so you get that creamy, expensive-looking blonde without any of the dryness.”
article_title: 2026’s Biggest Hair-Care Trends Start With Ingredients

trend_name: What's the difference between physical and chemical exfoliators?
description: This is one of the most frequently asked questions about exfoliators, and the difference is simple. Anything with small particles or grains that manually buff away buildup is considered physical. Chemical exfoliants include ingredients like AHAs and BHAs that dissolve buildup and oil at a cellular level, which is often gentler. "While both methods can be effective, I rely on chemical exfoliants to efficiently dissolve buildup without unnecessary abrasion," says Small. "However, the best approach often depends on your specific scalp type, sensitivity, and sensorial preference."
article_title: Scalp Buildup Is Sabotaging Your Good Hair Day Potential—8 Exfoliators Experts Love

trend_name: How do you know if you're over-exfoliating your scalp?
description: Every exfoliating product comes with very specific instructions on how and how often to use it. Still, everyone has a slightly different response to ingredients, so keeping tabs to make sure you're not over-exfoliating is a must. "Over-exfoliation often shows up as increased sensitivity, dryness, or irritation on the scalp," says Small. "You may also notice flaking that feels more reactive than buildup-related, or a tight, uncomfortable feeling after washing." He adds that a compromised scalp barrier can also lead to more oil production and skin shedding so finding a consistency that works for you is a major part of the process.
article_title: Scalp Buildup Is Sabotaging Your Good Hair Day Potential—8 Exfoliators Experts Love

You can do a lot with this length in terms of styling—giving you free rein to blow it out, curl it, straighten it, or let it air-dry—and it gives you plenty of room to inject personality. You want bangs? They instantly elevate a collarbone-length cut. Volume at the crown? Chic and encouraged—Raziuddin recommends taking avolumizing productto your roots for extra lift.

Customer review:“I LOVE THESE! My hair is so bouncy, it gives my fine hair SO MUCH VOLUME but still shiny! I washed my hair, only used Bumble and Bumble Pret Dry Shampoo (to cut down on future grease). With zero hairspray to set or any kind of product to keep the shape, the rollers made me look like I had 5x the amount of hair I actually have. It was almost too much and I had to run some oil through.”
article_title: It's Official: I've Never Achieved '90s Volume Faster Than With This Overlooked, Old-School Product

trend_name: How long should you wear hot rollers for?
description: The short answer? Until they’ve cooled. “That’s what actually sets the curl,” Stenson explains. “For most people, that’s around 15 to 30 minutes depending on hair thickness and density. Taking them out too early is one of the biggest reasons curls might fall flat quickly,” he adds.
